front: Novel: Dr. Slop's forceps flatten the protagonist's nose at birth; his father believes nose shape determines success
Novel: Uncle Toby's 'hobby-horse' is military earthworks; he reenacts the Siege of Namur with Corporal Trim using jackboots as mortars
Novel: a completely black page marks the death of Parson Yorick
Novel: the narrator takes three volumes to reach his own birth; digressions are the 'soul of reading'
Novel: 'have you forgotten to wind up the clock?' is asked at the moment of the protagonist's conception
Novel: the protagonist is accidentally circumcised by a falling window sash while urinating
Novel: Walter names his son 'Trismegistus' but a clerical error changes it; Hafen Slawkenbergius's On Noses is discussed
Novel: opens 'They order, said I, this matter better in France' and breaks off mid-sentence with the narrator grabbing a chambermaid
work: A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy

front: Author: coined the term 'Smelfungus' for Tobias Smollett, now meaning a complainer
work: A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy

front: Author: this novelist's skull was reinterred at Coxwold in 1969 after being recognized when his body was stolen from a grave
Author: correspondence with Ignatius Sancho in support of abolitionism helped bring Sancho to prominence
